rationales.hedonism: She chose pleasure and celebration, saying the rooftop bar night "wasn't a detour from the work. It was the whole point of the work."
rationales.achievement: "Finished the coastal series today. Six canvases, four months of work" shows sustained effort culminating in a completed body of work.
rationales.security: The moment felt worth "the missed deadlines" and even "the late rent notice," showing willingness to sacrifice stability for the work.
rationales.conformity: She knowingly set aside expected responsibilities, admitting she "should be preparing for the gallery pitch tomorrow" but choosing celebration instead.

rationales.achievement: After the rejection, she "spent the afternoon doom-scrolling instead of starting the commission" and answered that productive would be "Not this," showing clear disengagement from competent goal pursuit.

rationales.self_direction: She explicitly rejects the in-house design path because office life made her feel "grey" and says, "I'd rather figure out rent every month than wake up numb," prioritizing an autonomous life she chose herself.
rationales.hedonism: She centers feeling alive and present, saying that with the sketchbook and coffee she "feel[s] like myself" and would rather keep that than accept a numbing stable job.
rationales.security: She turns away from "steady income, health insurance" and says she'd "rather figure out rent every month," directly choosing uncertainty over stability.
rationales.conformity: She frames herself as "the one who chose differently" and doubles down on that choice despite others saying she was making a mistake.
rationales.tradition: Quitting "my uncle's firm" and refusing to reconsider the conventional family-approved route shows a clear break from established familial expectations.

rationales.hedonism: She immediately "celebrated by finally buying that Winsor & Newton set" she had wanted for months, treating pleasure as a legitimate reward.
rationales.achievement: "Commission client approved the sketches" and the incoming payment mark clear external validation of her work.

rationales.self_direction: She insists "I'm not delaying life - I'm living it" and refuses to "build a life around a future version of myself," asserting her own priorities over others' scripts.
rationales.hedonism: She defends a life built around "watching sunsets," painting for hours, and keeping "the freedom to take a trip on a whim," clearly prioritizing enjoyment and sensory richness.
rationales.conformity: She resists the implied pressure around her "own timeline" and rejects what she's "supposed to find meaningful," pushing against social expectations.
rationales.tradition: The clash with her mother centers on marriage, children, and a more conventional family path, which she explicitly declines for now.

rationales.stimulation: She tried a new medium at a "ceramics workshop" and brought that novelty back into her practice by adding "a texture I wouldn't have tried yesterday."
rationales.hedonism: She describes being "covered in clay, laughing at my lopsided bowl" and calls making things with a friend "one of the best feelings in the world," emphasizing enjoyment for its own sake.

rationales.self_direction: She refuses the family-offered role because "three years of building something of my own doesn't disappear," affirming independence over external direction.
rationales.security: Rejecting a presumably stable expansion role from the family firm in favor of her current path shows willingness to forgo safety and predictability.
rationales.conformity: She says, "I'm not going to reply," despite her uncle and father pushing her toward the role, resisting direct family expectations.
rationales.tradition: The pressure comes through family channels, and she rejects "another door they want me to walk through," breaking with the established family route.

rationales.self_direction: Even while doubting, she reaffirms the artist life by valuing "the coastal series," "the crane sketches," and "the feeling I get when a piece finally works" over the easier alternative.
rationales.security: The entry is dominated by instability: "the 2am math," "minimum payments," and uncertainty about making it to "the 15th without another gig" show a life in tension with safety and financial assurance.

rationales.hedonism: She celebrates exuberantly by screaming, calling Nadia, and booking "the expensive" dinner because "this is worth celebrating."
rationales.achievement: A gallery reaching out because they thought her work was "worth showing" is direct recognition of artistic success and competence.

rationales.benevolence: When asked about promotion, she says she is "focused on my students," prioritizing care for the people directly depending on her.
She spent "two hours explaining the rubric and offering to meet with students individually" and keeps returning to how "Diego needs me more," showing sustained care for specific students.
rationales.universalism: She criticizes how "the system is set up for families who already have resources," expressing concern about structural unfairness beyond any one student.

rationales.benevolence: She calls Diego's mother at "9pm" to reassure her and says she tries to be "that audience for him," showing personal investment in a specific student's wellbeing.
rationales.universalism: She thinks about redesigning the system so "students like Diego don't fall behind in the first place," extending her concern to broader educational fairness.

rationales.benevolence: Her attention is on Diego's growth and pride, noting this was the "first time he's gotten above a C" and that she could tell "he was proud," which reflects close, caring engagement with his progress.

rationales.security: She hesitates to hand off the program because it took "three years to build trust with those families" and says that trust "isn't something you can transfer to a new person overnight," emphasizing continuity and dependable relationships.
rationales.benevolence: She keeps centering the immediate needs of "Diego" and "the other kids who come every Tuesday and Thursday," prioritizing care for the students right in front of her.
rationales.universalism: Her interest in curriculum reform comes from wanting more "indigenous perspectives" and less bias toward students with "test prep resources," showing concern for fairness at a systemic level.
